sections.paragraph:
κρανίον, ου, τό (Hom. et al.; pap, LXX; ApcEsdr 5:24 p. 30, 28 Tdf.; Jos., Bell. 3, 245, Ant. 8, 390) skull κρανίου (epexegetic gen.) τόπος the place that is called (a) Skull as a transl. of Γολγοθᾶ (q.v.) Mt 27:33; Mk 15:22; J 19:17. Cp. Lk 23:33, where ‘Calvary’ of the KJV is not a NT place name, but the Lat. transl. of κ. (s. Goodsp., Probs. 89f). Schol. on Lucian 251, 20f Κρανίον· ἔστι τόπος ἐν Κορίνθῳ; Diog. L. 6, 77 ἐν τῷ Κρανείῳ τῷ πρὸ τῆς Κορίνθου γυμνασίῳ.—For other lit. s. on Γολγοθᾶ.—B. 213f. DELG. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

gen. gen. = genitive

transl. transl. = translate, translation

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

KJV KJV = King James version of the Bible—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Goodsp., Probs. Goodsp., Probs. = Problems of NT Translation—List 6

Schol Schol , on various authors—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 564
